---
title: "Week 11"
description: "Final Exam Review and Final Exam"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
---

```{=html}
<!--
Think of the weekly page as answering just five student questions:
    1.  What are we doing this week?
    2.  What do I need to read/watch before class?
    3.  What materials should I have open during class?
    4.  What is due (and when)?
    5.  Where do I go if I want to review later?
    -->
```

## Announcements

-   **The final exam opens Monday, March 16 at 3:00 PM and closes Friday, March 20 at 5:00 PM**
-   No new homework this week

## Overview

This is the final week of the course. Monday is dedicated to exam review — we will go through key concepts and R workflows from across the semester, with emphasis on Lessons 10–19. Bring your questions.

Wednesday's session is optional. We will cover a few topics that didn't fit in the regular semester — multiple linear regression, statistics for animal and bench science studies, and a look at a real collaboration — followed by open Q&A. Attendance is not required and will not affect your grade.

By the end of the week, you should feel confident in your ability to:

-   select the appropriate statistical test for a given research question and data structure,
-   apply the six-step hypothesis testing framework across all test types covered this semester,
-   check and interpret assumptions for parametric and nonparametric tests,
-   fit and interpret a simple linear regression model in R,
-   distinguish between confidence intervals for mean response and prediction intervals, and
-   communicate statistical results clearly in context.

## Readings (before class)

-   No required reading for Monday

## Materials

### Monday — Final Exam Review

-   **Topics**
    -   Exam logistics and structure
    -   First-half foundations: study design, Bayes' theorem, normal distributions and CLT
    -   Paired t-test
    -   One-way ANOVA and post-hoc testing
    -   Categorical data analysis: chi-squared and two-proportion tests
    -   Nonparametric tests: Wilcoxon and Kruskal-Wallis
    -   Correlation and simple linear regression
    -   Power, sample size, and choosing the right test
-   **Slides**
    -   Final Exam Review ([HTML](/lessons/22_final_review/22_final_review.qmd) | [PDF](/lessons/22_final_review/22_final_review.pdf) | [OneDrive](https://ohsuitg-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/g/personal/latour_ohsu_edu/IgCOtOYcIrovSKF4qK2E2G-dAYxn57PU_3BBTsgcOoepe1Q?e=eC1m6l))
    
-   **Recording**
    -   [Final Exam Review](https://ohsuitg-my.sharepoint.com/:v:/g/personal/latour_ohsu_edu/IQBsKTmQEjWnSIDkhWZJhOVuAWUM4qdRL_F-K0KVC9cSmfQ?nav=eyJyZWZlcnJhbEluZm8iOnsicmVmZXJyYWxBcHAiOiJPbmVEcml2ZUZvckJ1c2luZXNzIiwicmVmZXJyYWxBcHBQbGF0Zm9ybSI6IldlYiIsInJlZmVycmFsTW9kZSI6InZpZXciLCJyZWZlcnJhbFZpZXciOiJNeUZpbGVzTGlua0NvcHkifX0&e=I6bOHj)
-   **Muddy points**
    -   No muddy points this week.



------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Wednesday — Optional Session: Odds & Ends

-   Attendance is **optional** — this session will not affect your grade
-   Come with questions about the exam, specific concepts, or your own research
-   **Topics**
    -   Multiple linear regression: categorical predictors, adjusting for confounding
    -   Statistics for animal studies and bench science: mapping your experiments to your toolkit, experimental design, biological vs. technical replicates, power and sample size
    -   A real collaboration: linear mixed models applied to a UV radiation and skin biology study
    -   Open Q&A — exam questions, your own data, anything from the semester
-   **Slides**
    -   Multiple Linear Regression ([HTML](/lessons/23_odds_ends/23_mlr_categorical.qmd) | [PDF](/lessons/23_odds_ends/23_mlr_categorical.pdf) | [OneDrive](https://ohsuitg-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/g/personal/latour_ohsu_edu/IgCOtOYcIrovSKF4qK2E2G-dAYxn57PU_3BBTsgcOoepe1Q?e=eC1m6l))
    -   Statistics for Animal Studies ([HTML](/lessons/23_odds_ends/23_animal_studies.qmd) | [PDF](/lessons/23_odds_ends/23_animal_studies.pdf) | [OneDrive](https://ohsuitg-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/g/personal/latour_ohsu_edu/IgCOtOYcIrovSKF4qK2E2G-dAYxn57PU_3BBTsgcOoepe1Q?e=eC1m6l))
    -   A Real Collaboration ([HTML](/lessons/23_odds_ends/23_nevus_project.qmd) | [PDF](/lessons/23_odds_ends/23_nevus_project.pdf) | [OneDrive](https://ohsuitg-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/g/personal/latour_ohsu_edu/IgCOtOYcIrovSKF4qK2E2G-dAYxn57PU_3BBTsgcOoepe1Q?e=eC1m6l))
-   **Recording**
    -   Recording will be posted if the session is held

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Final Exam

**Available:** Monday, March 16 at 3:00 PM through Friday, March 20 at 5:00 PM

**Access:** [Sakai](https://sakai.ohsu.edu/)

**Time to budget:** 4–6 hours (you do not need to complete it in one sitting)

### Submission Requirements

Upload **both** files to Sakai:

1.  Your `.qmd` source file
2.  The rendered `.html` file

**File naming:** `Lastname_FirstInitial_Final` (e.g., `Smith_J_Final.qmd` and `Smith_J_Final.html`)

### Important Reminders

-   **Open book/notes:** You may use course materials, R documentation, and your notes
-   **No collaboration:** Work independently — no help from other students, AI assistants, or online forums
-   **Check before submitting:** Make sure your document renders to HTML without errors

### Resources

-   Final Exam Review ([HTML](/lessons/22_final_review/22_final_review.qmd) | [PDF](/lessons/22_final_review/22_final_review.pdf) | [OneDrive](https://ohsuitg-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/g/personal/latour_ohsu_edu/IgCOtOYcIrovSKF4qK2E2G-dAYxn57PU_3BBTsgcOoepe1Q?e=eC1m6l))
-   See [Schedule](schedule.qmd) for all lecture materials and homework

## Post-class check-in (attendance)

-   Please complete the post-class check-in after Monday's class meeting.
-   Wednesday attendance is optional and no check-in is required.
-   Link: [Post-class check-in](https://forms.gle/AMX1S1vCGEEfmwLY6)
